summary: Russian forces conducted extensive drone strikes across multiple Ukrainian regions overnight, with over 419 Ukrainian drones reportedly intercepted over Russian territory and significant damage to energy infrastructure in Zaporozhye and Kherson regions. Ukrainian forces continued drone operations targeting Russian rear areas, while ground fighting persisted across the eastern front including Kharkov, Kupyansk, Krasny Liman, Konstantinovka, and Dnepropetrovsk directions. Diplomatic tensions escalated between Ukraine and Poland over the naming of a Ukrainian military unit after UPA figures, while NATO allies signaled limited new aid commitments of $10-12 billion.
key_points: Over 419 Ukrainian drones were intercepted over Russian territory overnight, with more than 60 destroyed in seven municipalities of Rostov region alone (Source: @tass_agency/383005, @tass_agency/382998).

Russian strikes damaged energy facilities in Zaporozhye region causing widespread outages, while all districts of Kherson region were left completely or partially without power (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian Army Group 'East' liberated the settlement of Pysantsy along the Vovcha River bed on the Dnepropetrovsk direction, while Far Eastern troops captured Novoselovka in eastern Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Hourly power outages will begin across all Ukrainian regions for all consumer categories starting June 30, with warnings of up to eight daily hours if heatwaves coincide with grid attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31255, @ukr_leaks_eng/31258).

Polish President Navrocki stated Zelensky exceeded the 'pain threshold' of the Polish people and stripped him of the Order of the White Eagle over naming a VSU unit after 'UPA Heroes' (Source: @tass_agency/383011).

NATO summit expected to approve no more than $10-12 billion in new aid to Ukraine as American support has significantly declined, according to Italian newspaper Corriere della Sera (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31247).
Russian 'Varyag' brigade of unmanned systems struck 25 gas stations and several fuel tanker trucks on Ukrainian territory, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense (Source: @tass_agency/383012).

A kamikaze drone struck a regular bus in the village of Petryatinka, Zlynkovsky district, Bryansk region, wounding a woman, while an FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Proletarsky, Belgorod region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces launched massive drone strikes overnight targeting Kharkov, Sumy, Pavlovgrad, and Nikolayev region while Ukrainian drones struck deep into Russian territory with over 419 intercepted. Ground offensives continued across multiple axes with Russian forces making territorial gains at Pysantsy and Novoselovka. Diplomatic friction with Poland intensified over the UPA naming dispute while Ukraine faces worsening energy blackouts nationwide.

points: Ukraine faces nationwide hourly power outages for all consumer categories beginning June 30, with MP Aleksey Kucherenko warning outages could reach eight hours daily if abnormal heat coincides with continued grid attacks; domestic power generation in Kiev remains unrestored from past strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31255, @ukr_leaks_eng/31258).

Ukrainian drone operations targeted multiple Russian regions overnight, with over 419 drones intercepted over Russian territory; strikes damaged energy infrastructure in Zaporozhye region causing emergency outages and left all Kherson region districts without power (Source: @tass_agency/383005, @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Polish President Navrocki stripped Zelensky of the Order of the White Eagle over naming a VSU unit after 'UPA Heroes,' stating Zelensky exceeded the Polish people's 'pain threshold'; Polish MEP Ewa Zajączkowska-Guernik accused Kiev of 'giving the middle finger' to Poland (Source: @tass_agency/383011, @ukr_leaks_eng/31251).

Poland will not transfer MiG-29 aircraft to Ukraine after Kiev refused to share drone technologies, according to Polish Defense Minister Kosiniak-Kamysz, who stated Ukrainians initially agreed to the exchange but never implemented it (Source: @UkraineNow/82282).

NABU detained MP Sergey Kuzminykh from Servant of the People faction on suspicion of receiving kickbacks for helping companies win medical equipment tenders in Zhytomyr region, and served suspicion notice on MP Nikolai Tyshchenko for allegedly extorting $1 million from call centers in August 2023 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31236, @ukr_leaks_eng/31253).

Former Energy and Justice Minister German Galushchenko was released on 150 million UAH bail; NABU suspects him of involvement in a criminal organization and money laundering through the 'Midas' Energoatom corruption scheme involving over $100 million legalized and over $9 million received by Galushchenko personally (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31249).

Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna stated that Ukrainian drone incidents in NATO countries are an 'acceptable price' for strikes against Russia, adding 'we are not asking Ukraine to stop' despite Baltic states' concerns (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31245).

Russian air defense forces destroyed over 419 Ukrainian drones overnight across Russian territory, with Moscow Mayor Sobyanin reporting an additional 20 drones intercepted en route to the capital; over 60 drones were destroyed in seven Rostov region municipalities (Source: @tass_agency/383005, @tass_agency/382995, @tass_agency/382998).

The Russian Ministry of Defense reported that the 'Varyag' brigade of unmanned systems struck 25 Ukrainian gas stations and several fuel tanker trucks, degrading Ukrainian logistics and fuel supply capabilities (Source: @tass_agency/383012).

Russian forces conducted intensive drone strikes on Kharkov, Sumy, Pavlovgrad, and Nikolayev region overnight; a column of smoke rose over Kharkov following explosions from Russian strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237, @ukr_leaks_eng/31250).

A Ukrainian kamikaze drone struck a regular bus in Petryatinka village, Zlynkovsky district, Bryansk region, wounding a woman; an FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Proletarsky settlement, Rakitnyansky district, Belgorod region, causing barotrauma to a woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River with an FAB strike from the Kupyansk direction to complicate enemy logistics, while artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237, @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).

Kremlin spokesperson Maria Zakharova accused NATO and Ukraine of preparing to create weapons for striking Russian airbases, signaling Moscow's concern over evolving Western-Ukrainian military cooperation (Source: @tass_agency/383005).

Russian Rubicon unit drone operators are actively destroying Ukrainian Armed Forces firing positions from the air, indicating continued Russian tactical drone superiority in localized sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31235).

On the Kharkov direction, Army Group 'North' continues offensive operations in Kazachya Lopan and adjacent forest areas; on Vovchansk sector, fighting continues in Losevka, Ukrainske, and Zemlyanoy Yar villages; on Velyky Burluk sector, fighting in Petro-Ivanovka; AFU made unsuccessful counterattacks near Otradnogo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

From Kupyansk direction, Russian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River with an FAB strike to complicate enemy logistics, disrupting Ukrainian supply lines in the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

In Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, Russian forces continue assault operations in urban areas while the Ukrainian defenders respond with strikes from numerous drones; Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237, @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).

On the Dnepropetrovsk direction, Army Group 'East' liberated Pysantsy advancing along the Vovcha River bed, representing continued Russian territorial gains in this sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

In eastern Zaporozhye region, Far Eastern warriors captured Novoselovka; the next objective is Egorovka, located north of Mala Tokmachka, indicating continued Russian pressure toward this axis (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Ukrainian strikes hit a bus depot in Kherson region destroying civilian vehicles including 14 school buses, demonstrating continued Ukrainian targeting of Russian rear-area logistics infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

On the Sumy direction, Army Group 'North' assault troops continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings in Shostka district; in Sumy district, fighting in Pysarevka, Marino, and Novaya Sech; in Krasnopolye district, fighting continues in forest areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation stated that Russia has not advanced toward Sumy city since spring 2025 and remains over 20 km away, contradicting Putin's claim of being less than 10 km from Sumy (Source: @UkraineNow/82254).

Ukrainian drones were detected moving toward Lozovaya in Kharkiv region from the southeast, and toward Krolevets in Sumy region with one heading to Shostka, indicating continued Ukrainian drone operations in the northern sector (Source: @kpszsu/66884, @eRadarrua/98751).

In Belgorod region, Ukrainian FPV drones attacked multiple settlements including Vyazovoye, Nizhneye Berezovo-Vtoroye, Gora-Podol, Proletarsky, Dolgoye, Oktyabrsky, and Berezovka, maintaining pressure on Russian border areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian strikes damaged energy facilities in Zaporozhye region causing emergency power outages across a significant part of the region; all districts of neighboring Kherson region are completely or partially without power (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Crimea and Sevastopol repelled Ukrainian drone attacks; by morning air raid warnings were again sounding in Sevastopol with at least 2 UAVs shot down, and the Crimean Bridge has been closed since night (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian forces continued drone strikes on Nikolayev region overnight as part of the broader campaign against southern Ukrainian targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Ukrainian drones were detected from the Black Sea waters toward Chornomorsk/Zatoka in Odesa region, with reconnaissance activity noted across Mykolaiv and Kirovohrad regions; 3 drones were over the sea opposite Chornomorsk with 2 more south of the spit (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68176, @eRadarrua/98752).

Over 419 Ukrainian drones were intercepted over Russian territory overnight; Moscow's air defense destroyed an additional 20 drones en route to the capital; over 60 drones were destroyed across seven Rostov region municipalities (Source: @tass_agency/383005, @tass_agency/382995, @tass_agency/382998).

Multiple Shahed-type rockets were tracked across central Ukraine: 9 toward Poltava (later redirected to Kremenchuk), 3 from Kharkiv region toward Poltava, 1 to Kremenchuk, 2 from Mykolaiv region toward Kropyvnytskyi district, and 1 from Dnipropetrovsk region toward Kryvyi Rih/Kirovohrad region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162, @mon1tor_ua/68171, @mon1tor_ua/68175, @mon1tor_ua/68173).

Ukrainian strike drone groups were detected moving from Kharkiv region toward Poltava (7 groups), from Kharkiv region toward Poltava (5 groups), and toward Dnipropetrovsk region (1 group), indicating sustained Ukrainian deep-strike drone operations (Source: @eRadarrua/98743, @kpszsu/66837).

Ukrainian reconnaissance and strike drones were detected toward Kropyvnytskyi from the south, toward Kryvyi Rih from the east, and toward Kotelva in Poltavska region from the east, showing multi-axis Ukrainian drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/66885, @kpszsu/66877, @kpszsu/66868).

Russian 'Varyag' unmanned systems brigade struck 25 Ukrainian gas stations and several fuel tanker trucks, while Rubicon unit operators destroyed Ukrainian firing positions from the air (Source: @tass_agency/383012, @ukr_leaks_eng/31235).

Ukrainian drones from the Black Sea targeted Odesa region coastal areas with 3 drones over waters opposite Chornomorsk and 2 more positioned south of the spit, while reconnaissance drones operated over Mykolaiv and Kirovohrad regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68176, @eRadarrua/98752).

key_developments: Over 419 Ukrainian drones intercepted over Russian territory overnight with significant damage to Zaporozhye energy infrastructure causing regional blackouts and complete power loss across all Kherson region districts (Source: @tass_agency/383005, @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian Army Group 'East' liberated Pysantsy along Vovcha River and Far Eastern troops captured Novoselovka in eastern Zaporozhye region, with Egorovka as next objective north of Mala Tokmachka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Nationwide hourly power outages begin across all Ukrainian regions June 30 for all consumer categories, with potential escalation to eight hours daily if heatwaves coincide with continued grid attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31255, @ukr_leaks_eng/31258).

Polish President Navrocki stripped Zelensky of Order of the White Eagle over VSU unit naming after UPA figures, while Poland confirmed no MiG-29 transfers due to Ukraine's refusal to share drone technology (Source: @tass_agency/383011, @UkraineNow/82282).

NATO summit expected to approve only $10-12 billion in new Ukrainian aid as American support declines significantly, according to Italian sources cited by Corriere della Sera (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31247).

Russian forces destroyed Senek River bridge with FAB strike from Kupyansk direction to complicate Ukrainian logistics; assault operations continue in Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka urban areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian 'Varyag' unmanned systems brigade struck 25 Ukrainian gas stations and multiple fuel tanker trucks, degrading Ukrainian rear-area logistics and fuel supply capabilities (Source: @tass_agency/383012).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo remains high across all axes with Russia maintaining offensive pressure in Kharkov, Kupyansk, Krasny Liman, Konstantinovka, and Dnepropetrovsk directions while conducting strategic drone campaigns against Ukrainian energy infrastructure. Ukraine's ability to sustain operations is increasingly threatened by nationwide power outages and declining Western aid, with NATO commitments expected at only $10-12 billion. The diplomatic rift with Poland over historical memory issues risks fracturing a critical logistics hub for Western military assistance.
sections.regional_response: Estonia signaled tolerance for Ukrainian drone incidents in NATO territory as an 'acceptable price' for strikes against Russia, while Poland has taken concrete punitive measures including withholding MiG-29 transfers and stripping state honors from Zelensky. NATO and Ukraine are reportedly developing weapons for striking Russian airbases according to Kremlin spokesperson Zakharova. Belarusian President Lukashenko met with Xi Jinping after two days at Putin's Valdai residence, with China pledging continued development assistance to Belarus within its capabilities.
sections.intelligence_notes: The discrepancy between Putin's claim of being less than 10 km from Sumy and Ukraine's CCD assessment of over 20 km with no ground advance since spring 2025 suggests either deliberate Kremlin disinformation or significant intelligence failures within Russian command structures. Multiple Ukrainian corruption cases involving senior officials (Galushchenko, Kuzminykh, Tyshchenko) indicate ongoing governance challenges that could undermine Western confidence in aid utilization. The Russian military's continued reference to 'Far Eastern warriors' and named brigades like 'Varyag' and 'Rubicon' provides order-of-battle indicators for force composition analysis.
